retrieved.title: Studying safely
retrieved.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

retrieved.title: Studying safely
heading_path: Procedure in case of student accidents
retrieved.text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

Participating, asking questions, considering issues from different perspectives and interacting with others are central aspects of studying, but can also lead to conflict. retrieved.title: Studying safely
retrieved.text:
No smoking.

The only thing that is smoking here are our brains.

It is prohibited to handle flames or flammable liquids.

Drinking enough water will keep you fit and alert.

Access restrictions: stay away from the marked hazard zones.

Icon: Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces. (student while reading)

Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces.

Animals are not allowed inside. Exceptions apply to guide, assistance and other service dogs.

You can bring along your lucky charm.

Be quiet.

Science-based, respectful and appreciative discussions are expressly wanted.

Sports equipment may not be used inside the buildings.

Please train your mind as much as you want here.

It is prohibited to wear, carry or bring weapons of any kind to the University.

A razor-sharp mind is the only weapon allowed.

In addition to the security management team and the porters, you can also contact the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/), your [teachers](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) and the employees of the different organisational units in case of incidents.

retrieved.title: Services for graduates
retrieved.text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

retrieved.title: Confirmations for third parties
retrieved.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

"In case of force majeure (such as natural disasters, pandemics) or in case of comparable severe restrictions in emergency situations (such as war or war-like situations), the Rectorate may enact deviating regulations for students who are directly affected by the relevant situation and who are able to complete a partial achievement as part of a course with continuous assessment online or take an exam in a digital format, but who are unable to participate on site."

Statutes of the University of Vienna, Section Study Law, § 13i

The corresponding regulations currently in force are summarized below. The official text of the ordinance can be found in the [University Gazette](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/).

Regardless of this special rule, students with impairments are still entitled to request an [alternative mode of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/).

No corresponding regulations are currently in force.

reranked.text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

reranked.title: Studying safely
reranked.text:
Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [IT security: Tips for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/services/it-security/)
- [12 IT security commandments for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/it-news/article/news/12-it-security-gebote-fuer-nutzerinnen/?no_cache=1)
- [Password management](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password/)
- [Tips regarding the password](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password-tips/)

- [Social media and blog netiquette](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/netiquette/)
- [How to prevent Zoombombing](https://zid.univie.ac.at/it-news/artikel/news/keine-angst-vor-zoombombing/) (in German)

Participating, asking questions, considering issues from different perspectives and interacting with others are central aspects of studying, but can also lead to conflict. You can contact the following organisational units to seek help for yourself or for all conflict parties, or to establish a network that can help you in case of problems.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [ÖH Helpline](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/helpline/)
- [Psychological Counselling Service](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)
- [Accessible Studying Team of the University of Vienna](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/counselling/)
- [Sexual Harassment and Bullying Counselling Office](https://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/en/gender-equality-diversity/sexual-harassment-bullying-office/)
- [AKGleich - Equal Opportunities Working Party](https://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Arbiration Commission of the University of Vienna](https://schiedskommission.univie.ac.at/en/)

reranked.title: Studying safely
reranked.text:
No smoking.

The only thing that is smoking here are our brains.

It is prohibited to handle flames or flammable liquids.

Drinking enough water will keep you fit and alert.

Access restrictions: stay away from the marked hazard zones.

Icon: Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces. (student while reading)

Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces.

Animals are not allowed inside. Exceptions apply to guide, assistance and other service dogs.

You can bring along your lucky charm.

Be quiet.

Science-based, respectful and appreciative discussions are expressly wanted.

Sports equipment may not be used inside the buildings.

Please train your mind as much as you want here.

It is prohibited to wear, carry or bring weapons of any kind to the University.

A razor-sharp mind is the only weapon allowed.

In addition to the security management team and the porters, you can also contact the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/), your [teachers](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) and the employees of the different organisational units in case of incidents.

reranked.title: Services for graduates
reranked.text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)

reranked.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

reranked.title: Confirmations for third parties
reranked.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). selected.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

selected.title: Studying safely
selected.text:
Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [IT security: Tips for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/services/it-security/)
- [12 IT security commandments for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/it-news/article/news/12-it-security-gebote-fuer-nutzerinnen/?no_cache=1)
- [Password management](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password/)
- [Tips regarding the password](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password-tips/)

- [Social media and blog netiquette](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/netiquette/)
- [How to prevent Zoombombing](https://zid.univie.ac.at/it-news/artikel/news/keine-angst-vor-zoombombing/) (in German)

Participating, asking questions, considering issues from different perspectives and interacting with others are central aspects of studying, but can also lead to conflict. You can contact the following organisational units to seek help for yourself or for all conflict parties, or to establish a network that can help you in case of problems.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [ÖH Helpline](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/helpline/)
- [Psychological Counselling Service](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)
- [Accessible Studying Team of the University of Vienna](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/counselling/)
- [Sexual Harassment and Bullying Counselling Office](https://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/en/gender-equality-diversity/sexual-harassment-bullying-office/)
- [AKGleich - Equal Opportunities Working Party](https://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Arbiration Commission of the University of Vienna](https://schiedskommission.univie.ac.at/en/)

selected.title: Studying safely
selected.text:
No smoking.

The only thing that is smoking here are our brains.

It is prohibited to handle flames or flammable liquids.

Drinking enough water will keep you fit and alert.

Access restrictions: stay away from the marked hazard zones.

Icon: Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces. (student while reading)

Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces.

Animals are not allowed inside. Exceptions apply to guide, assistance and other service dogs.

You can bring along your lucky charm.

Be quiet.

Science-based, respectful and appreciative discussions are expressly wanted.

Sports equipment may not be used inside the buildings.

Please train your mind as much as you want here.

It is prohibited to wear, carry or bring weapons of any kind to the University.

A razor-sharp mind is the only weapon allowed.

In addition to the security management team and the porters, you can also contact the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/), your [teachers](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) and the employees of the different organisational units in case of incidents.

assemble.context:
[1] Studying safely
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

- [Security management](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/en/services/security/)
- [Threat management – non-violent university](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/violence-free-university-prevention-and-support)
- [House Rules](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/house-rules/)
- [Laboratory and Workshop Regulations](https://intra.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/upload/rrm/ANS/Downloads/Laborordnung/LABOR-und_WERKSTA%CC%88TTENORDNUNG_EN.pdf)

- [General Fire Safety Regulations](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/d_rrm/Downloads/2015_2016_Brandschutzordnung.pdf) (in German)
- [Procedure in case of student accidents](#c1215109)
- [Student’s Union (ÖH) insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/services/insurance-policy-austrian-students-union-oh)
- [Emergency text message](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

[2] Studying safely — Studying safely on-site. > Procedure in case of student accidents
What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393 31383 (for Vienna, Lower Austria, Burgenland).

You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).
